A little about me ↓
About me

I love bringing different perspectives to building.

I grew up in an immigrant family where hard work was a value, not a trait. Sports became the place I felt most like myself, and by 16 I was ranked top 500 in the world in tennis. An injury ended that chapter, but the athlete’s instinct to observe, adapt and keep going never left me.

I dropped out of NYU after an innovation challenge made me want to build *not just study* the future. Since then, I’ve moved between sales, product and founding companies, so I rarely see a problem from only one angle.

My approach to building is minimalist. I believe in cutting through the noise, taking a problem-first approach, and closely mirroring human workflows rather than building technology for the sake of it.

Get in touch → Problem-first · Human-centered · Minimalist
